rulings.Case Title: SIMA ZOKAEI VS. SALEM MANSOIR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: RESPONDENT SALEM MANSOIRS PETITION TO VACATE THE ARBITRATION AWARD
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 23, 2023 line 1. RESPONDENT SALEM MANSOIR'S PETITION TO VACATE THE ARBITRATION AWARD is DENIED. The petition is untimely. Even if the petition was timely, it lacks merit. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT PARKHURST VS. PEDRO'SMOVING SERVICES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 23, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANTS PEDRO'S MOVING SERVICES, INC. AND PEDRO HERMOSILLO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts in support of each element of the cause of action for Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. rulings.Case Title: BAYVIEW PROPERTY MANAGERS VS. FREDERICK DIXON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY/RESPONSE TO DISCOVERY
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 23, 2023 line 10. PLAINTIFF BAYVIEW PROPERTY MANAGERS' MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY, TO ESTABLISH ADMISSIONS FROM DEFENDANT, AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT is GRANTED, subject to opposition.
